channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Paris: How to avoid summer travel chaos ahead of RER, metro and tram line disruptions
item.description: Tourists and Parisians alike will have to contend with mass transport disruptions in the French capital and surrounding suburbs this summer due to planned major infrastructure works. Here’s what you need to know to navigate the RER train, metro, Transilien and tram services in July and August and how to plan alternative routes before travelling.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Luc MOUNIER

item.title: Belfast anti-immigrant riots show legacy of sectarian unrest
item.description: Anti-immigrant riots that broke out this week in Belfast, Northern Ireland, are part of a long history of conflict and sectarian divisions in a city marked by three decades of unrest known as the Troubles, according to residents and academics.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Albanian PM rallies support as Trump-linked resort row festers
item.description: As Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama appeared at a rally of jubilant supporters on Friday in Tirana, thousands were already filling a nearby boulevard, repeating daily calls for his resignation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France bids farewell to girl, 11, whose killing sparked outrage
item.description: Several hundred people gathered in southwestern France on Friday to bid farewell to an 11-year-old girl whose suspected murder sparked protests across the country and exposed failures in the country's judicial system.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Three Indian sailors died in US strike off Oman
item.description: Three Indian sailors have died in a U.S. military operation to halt a tanker off Oman as part of Washington's efforts to blockade Iran-linked shipping, Indian authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran peace deal looms while new military action flares near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran signaled on Friday that an agreement ​to end their war was close, with a senior U.S. administration official saying both sides had agreed on a text and that Washington expects to sign an initial deal in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Peru's leftist candidate Sanchez asks right-wing rival Fujimori to back vote recount
item.description: Peru's leftist presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez on Friday proposed a recount to his conservative rival Keiko Fujimori, citing alleged irregularities after votes coming in from abroad were tallied. Counting that continues at a sluggish pace has Fujimori ahead by a razor-thin margin.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Venezuela says leader of Tren de Aragua gang killed in 'joint operation' with US
item.description: The leader of the Tren de Aragua gang Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, for whom the US government had offered a reward of up to $5 million, was killed during a joint operation with the United States in southern Venezuela, the two countries announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Balogun's brace powers USA past Paraguay in Group D opener
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup co-hosts US thrash Paraguay in dominant Group D opener
item.description: Team USA made a commanding statement in their World Cup campaign, defeating Paraguay 4-1 in their Group D opener at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. Despite a late Paraguayan rally, the co-hosts' attacking prowess proved too much for La Albirroja, with Folarin Balogun scoring twice to set the tone.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Clinical USA dispatch Paraguay as Balogun and Reyna shine
item.description: Team USA put on a dominant display in their Group D opener, defeating Paraguay 4-1 at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. Folarin Balogun scored twice and Giovanni Reyna added a fourth as the USMNT made an emphatic statement on home soil, despite visa concerns and ticket prices that have overshadowed the tournament's opening days. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: Israeli and Palestinian groups urge world not to abandon two-state solution
item.description: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups appealed to the international community in France on Friday to keep pursuing a two-state solution, warning that the window for peace is narrowing. The call came as world powers gathered amid mounting concern over settlement expansion and violence in the occupied West Bank.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East war live: Lebanon reports new Israeli strikes in south after IDF warning
item.description: Lebanese state media reported fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday shortly after the Israeli army called on residents in 20 towns and villages, including many near Nabatieh city, to leave their homes. The US military said overnight it had “downed” several Iranian drones in the Strait of Hormuz as both sides insisted a peace deal was drawing closer. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Co-host Canada draws with Bosnia in Group B opener
item.description: Co-host nation Canada salvaged a 1-1 draw against Bosnia-Herzegovina on Friday night at Toronto's BMO Field, but missed a string of clear-cut chances that could have delivered a winning start to their Group B campaign.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: What are the African teams' chances?
item.description: A record number of African nations are at the 2026 World Cup, but South Africa’s opening defeat to Mexico has raised questions about the continent’s chances. Can African teams turn numbers into history and finally challenge for football’s biggest prize?
item.dc:creator: Clarisse FORTUNÉ

item.title: ‘I worked every day’: How David Hockney fell in love with France during the Covid lockdown
item.description: British artist David Hockney, who died at the age of 88 on Thursday, fell in love with the French countryside in the last few years of his life. He settled in Normandy in 2019, where he lived during the Covid-19 lockdown and was able to rededicate himself to nature over a year of solitude.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine's EU accession bid gains traction as Hungary lifts veto
item.description: The European Union will resume membership negotiations with Ukraine on Monday after Hungary's new government lifted a long-standing veto imposed by former prime minister Viktor Orban. EU leaders said all member states had agreed to open the first phase of accession talks with Ukraine and Moldova, marking a fresh show of support for Kyiv amid Russia's ongoing war.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Combating foreign digital interference: ‘France is among the top three in Europe’
item.description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu hosted political representatives from across party lines on Thursday to discuss the issue of electoral interference ahead of the country’s 2027 presidential election. French authorities are sounding the alarm over the rapid advances in artificial intelligence which they fear could make attempts to digitally manipulate the voting process from abroad – and consequently the electoral outcome – easier than ever before.
item.dc:creator: Grégoire SAUVAGE

item.title: Former Tory MP and military officer on Keir Starmer's 'rope-a-dope' week
item.description: The British prime minister is once again fighting for his political survival, on the back foot after his defence secretary and defence minister quit the government over complaints of a lack of military spending and sufficient commitment to keep Britain safe from the spectre of Russian attack.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Kosovo's 'Groundhog Day' politics
item.description: Right now, the path the EU and NATO faces another set back. There's been no functioning government, despite three elections in less in 16 months.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Iran, Ukraine, Belfast & Albania’s ‘Flamingo Revolution’
item.description: In a week that began with President Trump pronouncing that a ceasefire extension was so close with Iran, two or three days max, and that would only take an hour to finalise. Hours later Tehran downed a US Apache helicopter off the Gulf of Oman with the crew rescued from the sea, the attack reportedly an attempt to deter the US’s evolving efforts to increase air patrols that target Iranian drones in order to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Paris des Arts: Special edition in Prague
item.description: This week, Paris des Arts heads to the Czech Republic to visit Prague, a cultural capital and UNESCO World Heritage city. We meet one of the most provocative Czech artists of the international scene, David Cerny, who takes us inside his exhibition space – a former industrial site he's named the "Musoleum", a mashup of "museum" and "mausoleum".
item.dc:creator: Valérie FAYOLLE

item.title: Iran-linked group claims hack of FBI drones, threatens World Cup, monitor reports
item.description: An Iran-linked hacker group called Handala claimed to have breached FBI drones and threatened to target the 2026 World Cup, monitoring group SITE said Friday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: LGBTQ rights in Hungary: 'We are very hopeful that changes will be made'
item.description: The election of the Hungary's new centre-right prime minister, Peter Magyar, in April marked a particularly stinging defeat for Viktor Orban, whose government was widely regarded as one of the most anti-LGBTQ in the EU. Rights advocates expect legal changes but wonder about the timeline Magyar will adopt as the new prime minister remains cautious on divisive issues.
item.dc:creator: Simon FEISTHAUER FOURNET
